purpose: Students connect scale of 10 to familiar contexts where counting by 10s is natural.
visual:
Three real-world scenario images:
  - A: Counting money (stacks of 10 dollar bills)
  - B: Measuring height (ruler marked in 10s of centimeters)
  - C: Counting items one by one
guide: "Scale of 10 is useful when numbers are bigger. Where might you see counting by 10s outside of math class?"
prompt: "Which situations would work well with a scale of 10? Select all that apply."
student_action: Multi-select: A and B (both involve counting by 10s)
on_correct: "Money and measuring often use 10s. That's why scale of 10 feels natural for bigger amounts."
connection_guide_states_after_task: "You already know how to count by 10s. Bar graphs use the same skill you use with money and measuring."
